title: Analysis of murine lamina propria TH17 cells
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"34tkj","text":"IL-17-expressing CD4+ cells (TH17 cells) constitute a considerable proportion of lymphocytes in the intestinal lamina propria (LP), even in healthy mice kept under specific pathogen free conditions.  TH17 cells are rarely observed in the spleen, mesenteric lymph node, or Peyer’s patches.   Germ-free mice or antibiotics (vancomycin and metronidazole)-treated mice show marked reductions in numbers of LP TH17 cells; thus, intestinal commensal bacteria provide a particular environment for the development of LP TH17 cells.\r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"nrtoq","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"odcjm","text":"\r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"s9wav","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"w5tmt","text":"This protocol describes the procedure to isolate LP lymphocytes and to assay for IL-17 expression by intracellular cytokine staining.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Open intestines longitudinally, and wash in PBS to remove faecal content.
Shake in HBSS containing 5 mM EDTA for 20 min at 37°C.

After removal of epithelial cells and fat tissue, cut the intestines into small pieces and incubate with RPMI1640 containing 4% FBS, 1 mg/ml collagenase type II, 1 mg/ml dispase and 40 µg/ml DNase I for 1 h at 37°C in a shaking water bath.
Wash the digested tissues with HBSS containing 5 mM EDTA, resuspend in 5 ml of 40% Percoll (GE Healthcare) and overly on 2.5 ml of 80% Percoll in a 15-ml Falcon tube.
Centrifuge the samples at 2,000 rpm for 20 min at room temperature.
Collect LP lymphocyte from the interface of the Percoll gradient and wash with PBS containing 0.5% BSA and 2 mM EDTA) or RPMI1640. The cells can be used immediately for further experiments.
Incubate LP lymphocytes with 50 ng/ml PMA, 5 µM Calcium Ionophore A23187 and Golgistop in complete media at 37°C for 4 h.
Incubate the cells with a corresponding cocktail of fluorescently labeled antibodies, such as anti-CD4 and anti-TCR-β, for 20 min at 4°C.
Permeabilize the cells with Cytofix/Cytoperm solution for 20 min at 4°C.
Incubate cells with fluorescently labelled cytokine antibodies, e.g. anti-IL-17 and anti-IFN-γ, for 20 min at 4°C.
Wash cells with PBS containing 0.5% BSA and 2 mM EDTA buffer.

ATP drives lamina propria TH17 cell differentiation by K Atarashi, J Nishimura, T Shima, Y Umesaki, M Yamamoto, M Onoue, H Yagita, N Ishii, R Evans, K Honda & K Takeda published in Nature on 01 Jul, 2008 https://dx.doi.org/10.1038/nature07240

Figure 1. Lymphocytes were isolated from the lamina propria of the large and small intestine of SPF mice, and stimulated with PMA and calcium ionophore for 4 h. After the stimulation, cells were permeabilized, stained for CD4, IFN-&#x3B3; and IL-17, and analysed by FACS. Representative dot plots gated on CD4+ cells are shown. Numbers indicate the percentages of cells in the quadrants.
